krayin-crm.name: Krayin CRM

pros: Fully open source (MIT license) and free to self-host
Large, active GitHub project with 23,000+ stars and recent commits
Covers sales, marketing automation, and support in one CRM
Extensible with official extensions for VoIP, multi-tenant SaaS, and Google Workspace sync
cons: Self-hosting on Laravel/PHP requires setting up and maintaining your own server
No fixed first-party managed-cloud pricing; hosted setups are sold separately through Webkul's Cloudkul service and third-party hosts
Industry-specific solution templates and extensions add complexity to evaluate

faqs.answer: Yes, Krayin is fully open source and free to download and self-host.
faqs.question: Is Krayin CRM free?

faqs.answer: Krayin is maintained by Webkul as an open-source project.

faqs.answer: Krayin is built on Laravel (PHP).

faqs.answer: Webkul offers Cloudkul managed hosting setup on AWS, GCP, and Azure, and third-party hosts also offer Krayin hosting plans.

webhook-site.name: Webhook.site
webhook-site.short_description: Webhook.site generates free, unique URLs and email addresses to instantly inspect, test, and automate incoming HTTP requests and emails.

features.description: Receive and inspect incoming HTTP requests in real time at a unique URL.

features.description: Generates unique email addresses for testing email integrations.

features.description: Captures and inspects DNS queries for testing.
features.title: DNS monitoring (DNSHook)

features.description: Re-executes previously captured requests.

features.description: A drag-and-drop workflow builder with AI capabilities for automating actions on incoming requests.

features.description: Connects to Google Sheets, Slack, AWS S3, Dropbox, SFTP, databases, and JavaScript.
features.title: Native integrations

pros: Free tier requires no signup for quick webhook testing
Supports both webhook and email testing use cases
Includes a built-in workflow/automation builder
Native integrations with common tools like Slack, S3, and Google Sheets
Request replay simplifies debugging
cons: Free tier is limited to 100 requests per unique URL and URLs expire
Permanent addresses and account protection require a paid plan
Advanced features like SSO, custom domains, and white-labeling are reserved for paid tiers

faqs.answer: Yes, Webhook.site offers a free tier limited to 100 requests per unique URL, with URLs that expire after a set period.
faqs.question: Is Webhook.site free?

faqs.answer: Paid plans start from $7.50/month and include unlimited requests and emails, permanent protected addresses, and up to 100,000 request history.

faqs.answer: Yes, Webhook.site generates unique email addresses so you can capture and inspect test emails.

faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a drag-and-drop workflow builder with AI capabilities for automating actions on captured requests.
faqs.question: Does Webhook.site support automation?

features: Up to 100 requests per unique URL
URLs expire after a set period

arc.name: Arc
arc.short_description: Arc is a cash management and capital markets platform for technology companies, offering yield, cards, and debt financing.

features.description: Treasury dashboard for managing operating cash and brokerage balances in one place.

faqs.answer: Arc offers a free Essentials tier, with paid Premium and Enterprise tiers unlocking higher yield, more cashback, and dedicated support.

faqs.answer: Yes, Axos Financial announced a definitive agreement in July 2026 to acquire Arc Technologies, Inc.

features: Up to 4.12% net yield on brokerage
Core cash management and treasury features
Up to 4.52% net yield on brokerage
Up to 4.57% net yield on brokerage

stoplight.name: Stoplight
stoplight.short_description: Stoplight is a design-first platform for building, documenting, mocking, and governing OpenAPI-based APIs, now operating under SmartBear.

features.description: A visual editor with comprehensive OpenAPI and JSON Schema support lets teams design and prototype APIs without writing complex syntax by hand.
features.title: Visual API Editor

features.description: Mock servers are generated automatically, stay up-to-date with the API design, and support request and response validation for testing.
features.title: Automatic Mock Servers

features.description: API documentation is generated automatically alongside design work, with a live preview available directly in the editor.
features.title: Interactive Documentation

features.description: Built-in and custom style guides enforce design consistency, with shared style guides available on Pro and Enterprise plans to promote governance across an API portfolio.
features.title: Style Guides & Governance

features.description: Schemas and components can be reused across multiple APIs, with centralized updates that propagate to all dependent APIs.
features.title: Component Reusability

features.description: Integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure so teams can work with API design artifacts directly in their existing Git workflow.
features.title: Git Sync

features.description: The Pro Team plan adds LDAP/SAML SSO, workspace groups, and activity logs for larger organizations.
features.title: SSO & Activity Logs

features.description: Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral (linting), Prism (mocking), and Elements (documentation).
features.title: Open Source Tooling

pros: Free perpetual plan available for a single user and project
All paid plans include a 14-day free trial
Visual editor lowers the barrier to working with OpenAPI and JSON Schema
Built-in and shared style guides support API governance across teams
Reusable components reduce duplication across an API portfolio
Open-source tools (Spectral, Prism, Elements) are available alongside the hosted platform
cons: Paid plans charge per additional user beyond the included seats, which can increase cost for larger teams
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published on the site
Free plan is limited to 1 user and 1 project
Now operates as part of SmartBear following its 2023 acquisition, rather than as a fully independent company

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight offers a Free plan for $0 that includes 1 user, 1 project, the API editor, visual designer, interactive docs, and instant/local mock servers.
faqs.question: Is there a free plan for Stoplight?

faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans (Basic, Startup, Pro Team) include a 14-day free trial.
faqs.question: Does Stoplight offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight's website and pricing remain active under the Stoplight brand, though the company now operates as part of SmartBear Software following an acquisition announced in 2023.
faqs.question: Is Stoplight still an active, standalone product?

faqs.answer: Stoplight integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure for Git-based API design workflows.
faqs.question: What Git providers does Stoplight integrate with?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral for linting, Prism for mocking, and Elements for documentation.
faqs.question: Does Stoplight provide open-source tools?

espocrm.name: EspoCRM
espocrm.short_description: EspoCRM is an open-source CRM for managing contacts, companies and opportunities, available self-hosted or as a paid cloud service.

features.description: Track contacts, companies, projects and opportunities in one place.

30-day free trial available on cloud plans

faqs.answer: Yes, EspoCRM cloud plans include a 30-day free trial.

faqs.answer: Yes, EspoCRM can be installed on your own Windows, macOS or Linux server, including via Docker.

features: Dedicated IP, private server and custom domain

features: You provide and manage your own infrastructure
Best for technical teams with in-house IT
Hosting, security, updates and backups included
Best for teams without in-house IT resources
Configuration and one day of training
Best for most teams getting started
20% discount when bundled with managed hosting

quickmail.name: QuickMail
quickmail.short_description: Cold outreach platform for multi-channel email and LinkedIn campaigns with deliverability monitoring.

features.description: Combine email and LinkedIn connection automation in the same outreach sequence, with ICP filtering.
features.title: Multi-channel campaigns

features.description: Includes email warming, sender rotation, and blacklist monitoring to protect sending reputation.
features.title: Deliverability monitoring

features.description: QuickMail's Inbox Rotation technology distributes sends across multiple mailboxes.
features.title: Inbox Rotation

features.description: Manage email and LinkedIn replies from a single unified inbox.
features.title: Unified inbox

features.description: An AI feature to rewrite outreach message copy.
features.title: Reword with AI

features.description: Native two-way sync with HubSpot and Pipedrive, plus Zapier and API access.
features.title: CRM sync

features.description: Advanced analytics and automated deliverability insights for tracking campaign performance.
features.title: Campaign analytics

pros: Combines email and LinkedIn outreach in one platform
Built-in deliverability tools like email warming, sender rotation, and blacklist monitoring
Native two-way sync with HubSpot and Pipedrive
14-day free trial with no automatic charge after it ends
Won G2 Best Estimated ROI and High Performer badges in Spring 2024
cons: Entry plan caps uploaded contacts at 1,000 and emails sent at 5,000/month
API and webhook access are reserved for higher-tier Growth and Agency plans
Additional workspaces on the Agency plan cost extra per workspace

faqs.answer: QuickMail is a cold outreach platform for running multi-channel email and LinkedIn campaigns to generate leads.
faqs.question: What is QuickMail?

faqs.answer: QuickMail's Starter plan is $49/month, Growth is $99/month, and Agency is $299/month, each with a 14-day free trial.
faqs.question: How much does QuickMail cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, QuickMail has native two-way sync with HubSpot and Pipedrive, plus Zapier and API access on higher plans.
faqs.question: Does QuickMail integrate with a CRM?

faqs.answer: QuickMail states it has operated since 2014.
